formosa-font/01.webp: A white booklet with a minimalist cover features a delicate rose-gold botanical illustration of a stylized flower above bilingual typography reading "FORMOSA FONT 福爾摩沙" and "原生植物歐文字體設計" in a refined mix of Latin and Chinese characters set against a dark background.
formosa-font/02.webp: Two cream-colored booklets displayed side by side against a black background, featuring elegant botanical line illustrations of a rose and ornamental flora rendered in warm brown ink. The covers showcase the "Formosa Font" typeface with bilingual typography pairing Latin letterforms and Traditional Chinese characters, reflecting the project's theme of Taiwan's native plant-inspired type design.
formosa-font/03.webp: A cream-colored booklet for the Formosa Font project displayed flat and upright against a black background, featuring a delicate botanical line illustration at the top and bilingual typography pairing elegant serif Latin lettering with Chinese characters for the title "FORMOSA FONT 原生植物歐文字體設計."
formosa-font/04.webp: Two cream-white booklets for "Formosa Font" are displayed on a dark surface, their covers featuring a delicate botanical illustration of a rose alongside bilingual typography that mixes elegant serif Latin lettering with traditional Chinese characters.
formosa-font/05.webp: The spine of the Formosa Font book stands upright against a dark background, showing the title "FORMOSA FONT" in Latin letters at the top and vertical Chinese text below. The minimal white cover contrasts sharply with the black backdrop, highlighting the clean typographic layout.
formosa-font/06.webp: A cream-white booklet cover set against a dark background, featuring an ornate gold botanical line illustration above the bilingual title "Formosa Font / 福爾摩沙 FONT" and the subtitle "8 Typefaces / 原生植物歐文字體設計," rendered in elegant gold serif and sans-serif lettering.
formosa-font/07.webp: The back cover of a slim white booklet photographed against a dark background, featuring the gold-foil "FORMOSA" logotype flanked by ornate flourishes, with bilingual body text in Latin and Chinese describing Taiwan's endemic plant life and the typeface's design concept.
formosa-font/08.webp: Front and back covers of the Formosa Font booklet displayed against a dark background, featuring an ornate gold botanical emblem and the title "FORMOSA FONT 8 TYPEFACES" in elegant serif typography alongside Chinese characters, printed on cream-white stock.
formosa-font/09.webp: A cream-colored booklet for Formosa Font displayed front and back against a dark background, featuring elegant gold foil typography that blends Latin letterforms with Chinese characters and a delicate botanical line illustration on the cover.
formosa-font/10.webp: A printed booklet titled "Formosa Font — 8 Typefaces" lies at an angle on a dark surface, its cream-white cover featuring elegant serif Latin lettering alongside vertical Chinese text, with a delicate botanical line illustration in the upper right corner.
formosa-font/11.webp: An open book spread with cream-colored pages, featuring the ornate "FORMOSA" logotype surrounded by decorative calligraphic flourishes printed in dark brown ink. Body text in an elegant serif typeface reads about the design combining Latin typefaces with Taiwan's native plant life.
formosa-font/12.webp: A soft-cover book photographed at an angle against a dark background, showing the spine and front cover with "FORMOSA FONT" in serif lettering alongside Chinese characters, printed on cream-white paper with elegant black typography.
formosa-font/13.webp: A close-up of an open book spreads across a dark surface, its white pages bearing the text "FORMOSA FONT" in delicate serif type alongside flowing, ink-drawn calligraphic flourishes that resemble spiraling botanical forms. The soft-focus photography and dramatic low-key lighting emphasize the refined, hand-crafted quality of the printed design.
formosa-font/14.webp: An open book spread displaying the Formosa Font title page with ornate calligraphic lettering and decorative flourishes on the left, alongside a right page listing Latin and Chinese names of Taiwan's native plant species used as typographic inspiration.
formosa-font/15.webp: An open specimen book displays the full glyph set of an ornate calligraphic typeface, with uppercase and lowercase letterforms arranged in rows across cream-white pages against a dark background. The elegant script characters feature elaborate swashes and fine hairline strokes, shown at multiple weights and sizes in a clean typographic layout.
formosa-font/16.webp: An open spread of The Book of Formosa Font showing elegantly typeset specimen pages with ornate serif and calligraphic typefaces, floral ornaments, and structured font information layouts printed in black on cream paper against a dark background.
formosa-font/17.webp: A close-up photograph of a thick white-paged book opened to reveal its cover, displaying an elegant serif typeface with a stylized "A" and the word "FONT" in refined letterforms. The shallow depth of field and dark background emphasize the crisp, high-contrast black typography against the bright white paper.
formosa-font/18.webp: An open book photographed at a shallow depth of field, showing white pages printed with elegant cursive script typography in black ink, including the word "Lycopodium" prominently on the right page. The soft-focus composition emphasizes the graceful letterforms of the Formosa Font against crisp white paper.
foucault-book-binding/01.webp: A flat-lay arrangement of redesigned book covers for Foucault's works, featuring bold geometric color-block compositions in pastel pink, lavender, peach, and sage green against a dark background. The covers mix Chinese and English typography with structured grid layouts, referencing titles such as *The Order of Things* and *The Archaeology of Knowledge*.
foucault-book-binding/02.webp: A flat-lay spread of multiple book cover designs for Foucault's works, featuring a mix of pastel pink, teal, yellow, and black panels with large-scale Chinese and Latin typography arranged in dynamic diagonal compositions. The designs combine dense body text, bold ideographic characters, and classical photographic imagery to evoke themes of knowledge, archaeology, and order.
foucault-book-binding/03.webp: Multiple stacked book sets arranged in a repeating isometric pattern against a dark background, each set featuring the same cover design with geometric blocks of teal, pink, yellow, and white, bilingual typography in Latin and Chinese characters, and the series title "épistémè" in bold serif type.
foucault-book-binding/04.webp: Two Foucault book designs are laid diagonally on a dark surface — the upper book features dense newsprint-style text with a yellow panel printed with large pink Chinese characters, while the lower book displays a geometric color-block cover in salmon, teal, and grey with the title "The Order of Things" in clean sans-serif type alongside Chinese typography.
foucault-book-binding/05.webp: A collection of book spines and pages fanned together, displaying mixed typography in both Chinese and French/English — including the title "The Order of Things" in bold sans-serif — with pastel pink, green, and neutral tones creating a layered, editorial composition. Dense text columns and structured layouts evoke Foucault's themes of knowledge systems and disciplinary order.
foucault-book-binding/06.webp: Three white ribbon bookmarks printed with layered Chinese characters and bilingual text are arranged diagonally against a dark background, their typographic surfaces combining dense East Asian script with Latin text in a structured, grid-like pattern that evokes archival order and textual authority.
foucault-book-binding/07.webp: A flat-lay arrangement of multiple book covers and a rolled poster spread across a dark surface, featuring bold geometric color blocking in pink, yellow, lavender, and grey alongside large-scale Chinese and Latin typography. The designs for Foucault's works blend bilingual typographic systems with photographic fragments, creating a structured yet dynamic visual identity across the series.
foucault-book-binding/08.webp: A flat-lay arrangement of multiple book covers and printed materials on a dark surface, featuring a coordinated design system in pastel pink, yellow, green, and white with bold Chinese and English typography, including the title *The Order of Things* alongside geometric shapes and dense typographic layouts referencing Foucault's texts.
foucault-book-binding/09.webp: A flat-lay arrangement of book covers and a spine against a dark background, featuring embossed Traditional Chinese characters in muted beige tones. The textured surfaces contrast with a densely typeset spine in the center, creating a layered composition that evokes archival and classical print traditions.
foucault-book-binding/10.webp: Two book covers for *The Archaeology of Knowledge* by Michel Foucault displayed side by side against a dark background, featuring a geometric pastel color-block layout in soft pink, lavender, yellow, and grey with layered Chinese and Latin typography printed in contrasting white and black.
foucault-book-binding/11.webp: Two book cover designs for *The Archaeology of Knowledge* stand side by side against a dark background — the front cover features bold pink, pale blue, and cream geometric panels with large Chinese characters and layered bilingual typography, while the spine displays dense horizontal striped patterning with Chinese text and coil binding hardware.
foucault-book-binding/12.webp: A book design system for Foucault's *The Archaeology of Knowledge* featuring an unfolded dust jacket alongside a book and bookmark, using a geometric grid of pink, pale blue, gold, and grey panels. Bold Chinese characters and bilingual Latin typography are layered over collaged newsprint textures and archival imagery, conveying the themes of knowledge, discontinuity, and contingency.
foucault-book-binding/13.webp: An open book spread displaying a bilingual table of contents — English on the left page over a warm yellow background, Chinese on the right — with a bold graphic inset at the bottom combining a monochrome skull image, a hot-pink diagonal block, and large Chinese characters reading "目次" (contents), set against a dark studio backdrop.
foucault-book-binding/14.webp: An open book spread featuring bilingual text layout in English and Traditional Chinese, with a warm gold-toned left page and a white right page. A bold pink and grey geometric section divider marks an "Introduction" chapter, with large Chinese characters overlaid in a structured typographic composition.
foucault-book-binding/15.webp: Two book covers for Foucault's *The Order of Things* displayed side by side against a dark background, featuring bold geometric color-blocking in pink, black, and deep teal, with a bilingual typographic layout mixing large Chinese characters (事物秩序 / 認識型) and Latin text including the word "episteme" in oversized italic type.
foucault-book-binding/16.webp: Two book covers for Foucault's *The Order of Things* stand side by side against a dark background — the front cover features a geometric pastel design in pink, black, and white with bold Chinese characters and bilingual English/French titling, while the spine showcases a dense vertical arrangement of white Chinese text on a striped pattern with metallic staple-like details.
foucault-book-binding/17.webp: A flat-lay of a book binding design for Michel Foucault's *The Order of Things*, featuring a geometric grid layout with bold diagonal panels in salmon pink, dark green, white, and black. The composition layers bilingual Chinese and English typography with a reproduced classical painting fragment and structured table-of-contents text, creating a visually dense, editorial aesthetic.
foucault-book-binding/18.webp: An open book spread featuring a geometric diagram divided into quadrants using intersecting diagonal and orthogonal lines, with sections in white, deep green, and soft peach tones. Chinese and English typography label each zone, with large vertical Chinese characters and "Seventeenth and Eighteenth Centuries" anchoring the lower-right corner against a dark shadow-cast background.
foucault-book-binding/19.webp: An open book spread photographed on a dark surface, featuring a structured layout with a deep green panel containing a white node diagram on the left page alongside a grey diagram with handwritten-style English annotations, dense vertical Chinese body text, and a salmon-pink geometric triangle accent marking a chapter opening. The typographic design blends Chinese and Latin scripts with diagrammatic infographics, reflecting the analytical visual language of the Foucault book-binding project.
foucault-book-binding/20.webp: Multiple copies of the book series are stacked in a diagonal arrangement against a dark background, showcasing the spines and covers featuring a pastel color palette of pink, yellow, green, and white with bold bilingual typography in both Chinese characters and Latin script.
patterned-glass-notebook/01.webp: A collection of handcrafted notebooks and boxes with wood-framed covers featuring textured patterned glass surfaces — including geometric mosaic, star-etched, and rippled or crackled glass designs — arranged in a moody studio still life alongside rough stone props.
patterned-glass-notebook/02.webp: Three notebooks and a small box with patterned glass covers — featuring diamond-grid, cracked, and ribbed glass textures framed in warm bronze — are arranged on raw concrete blocks against a neutral grey background.
patterned-glass-notebook/03.webp: Close-up of the notebook's top corner, highlighting a weathered brass-toned metal spine with an embossed circular logo, set against a textured glass cover with a raindrop-like bubble pattern.
patterned-glass-notebook/04.webp: A close-up of the packaging box's corner, revealing layered notebook spines with gold-accented edges stacked inside, while the box exterior displays an intricate geometric tile pattern in dark teal and black with embossed badge-style typography.
patterned-glass-notebook/05.webp: A sculptural product still life stacking the patterned-glass notebook and its box alongside raw concrete and stone props, with the diamond-tilted lid revealing a faceted mirror-glass surface and warm gold trim. Kraft-toned label stickers bearing vintage-style typography and icons are layered on the front of the box, contrasted against the cool grey tones of the patterned glass panels below.
patterned-glass-notebook/06.webp: A styled still-life arrangement of multiple notebooks and gift boxes stacked and propped at dynamic angles, featuring covers and interiors clad in gold, silver, and dark wood-toned materials with geometric tile and mosaic patterns. The composition highlights the tactile, layered surfaces and the interplay of metallic and earthy textures across the full product range.
patterned-glass-notebook/07.webp: Multiple laser-etched metal bookmark or template cards featuring geometric and organic glass-pattern engravings are displayed upright inside a dark industrial box, propped on a rough concrete block with a stack of notebooks fanned beneath it.
patterned-glass-notebook/08.webp: An overhead flat-lay of multiple rectangular boxes with warm gold frames, each lid featuring a distinct silver embossed pattern — including floral, geometric, snowflake, honeycomb, and linear textures — arranged in a diagonal grid against a muted teal-green background.
patterned-glass-notebook/09.webp: Five small framed panels stand side by side against a softly draped grey-green backdrop, each displaying a distinct patterned glass texture — from floral and star motifs to vertical ribbing, a grid mosaic, and shattered fragments — all set in warm gold-toned wooden frames.
patterned-glass-notebook/10.webp: A close-up of a kraft-toned notebook standing upright, its spine and cover featuring embossed diamond lattice patterns in warm gold against dark brown. A debossed label reads "Toned Gray" with product details, and two die-cut bookmarks with geometric embossing peek out from the top.
patterned-glass-notebook/11.webp: Four notebooks fanned open and arranged symmetrically, showcasing covers with a dark marbled glass texture framed in gold, with one spine featuring an intricate blue geometric tile pattern, all set against a soft grey draped background.
patterned-glass-notebook/12.webp: Two gold-foil notebook spines are photographed diagonally against a muted teal-grey background, their textured metallic surfaces catching dramatic light and shadow. Each spine features a circular stamp imprint with "notebook" lettering, one inverted relative to the other, creating a mirrored composition.
patterned-glass-notebook/13.webp: Close-up of a dark textured notebook cover featuring an embossed geometric pattern of sharp, multi-pointed star motifs connected by fine diagonal lines, rendered in a metallic silver finish against a deep charcoal surface.
patterned-glass-notebook/14.webp: Close-up of the notebook's back cover details under warm, raking light, showing a circular brown label reading "notebook — Blank Sketch Paper — 320 pages" alongside a stamp-shaped vignette of the patterned glass motif and a "PATTERNED GLASS" text badge, all printed in white on a warm brown ground.
patterned-glass-notebook/15.webp: A gold-toned notebook back cover displaying product specification badges — including "Toned Gray," "100% Recycled Paper," and "Blank Sketch Paper 320 pages" — photographed through ribbed glass that refracts the image with vertical stripe distortions and soft shadow accents.
patterned-glass-notebook/16.webp: Two embossed kraft-paper bookmarks featuring patterned glass-inspired geometric textures — floral lattice, star motifs, and irregular stone — are arranged diagonally against a moody sky backdrop, with "Bookmark" printed in a handwritten script alongside a small book icon.
